MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nicholas W. Van Aelstyn, Esq. of Beveridge & Diamond, (415-262-4000), for Total Outdoor Corp., Plaintiff and opposing party.
No appearance by Defendants and moving parties.

Plaintiff's counsel informs the Court that he complies with the Tentative Ruling Rule and further informs opposing counsel that he will clarify or argue in open court the issue on sanctions. Court and counsel addresses sanctions request briefly.

Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard brief oral argument on sanctions, adopts the tentative ruling and takes the sanctions issue under submission, as follows: DEFENDANTs POSTING ACCESS REVENUE, LLC, KEVIN HICKS, CHRIS GIOUZELIS, CATHY GIOUZELIS, PETER PRONGOS, PANAGIOTA PRONGOS, RAYMOND REUDY and ADVERTISING DISPLAY SYSTEMS 1, LLC's Motion To Set Aside, or in the alternative renewed Motion For Summary Judgment is denied. Mr. Kernan's multiple declarations do not support existence of a triable issue of fact regarding permission to use the roof especially in light of Peter Progos admissions. Moving party had an opportunity to respond to Mr. Kernan's second declaration (February 6, 2014) at the hearing. Other than raising an objection to this rebuttal declaration, Plaintiff did not seeks any relief, such as, for example, continuance for additional discovery. Objections to Mr. Kernan's third declaration (August 27, 2014) are sustained. Renewed motion for Summary judgment as to trespass is denied. Moving party failed to sustained burned under CCP 1008(b). No new facts, circumstances or law presented. Mr. Kernan's third declaration and alleged lack of valid permit do not constitute new facts or circumstances. 1936 case is not new law.. Plaintiff's counsel submits proposed order in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
